pr.title: Begin split up of large ft code file, reorganized character files
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large fighter assembly organization PR. It moved many per-character ft assembly files into asm/melee/ft/chara/ with 100% similarity renames, split a very large asm/melee/ft/code_800804EC.s file into topical ft assembly units, and updated obj_files.mk so the build used the new file layout. The PR body explicitly says this was only the beginning: the source remained large and needed further splitting.
what_changed: Split large fighter assembly content out of asm/melee/ft/code_800804EC.s; that file shows 22433 deleted lines and only 1 added line in the changed-file summary.
Created new topical fighter assembly files under asm/melee/ft/: ftcoll.s, ftcommon.s, ftanim.s, ftparts.s, ftaction.s, ftcamera.s, code_8007C114.s, and code_8007C630.s.
Added asm/melee/ft/code_8007C114.s covering the address range 0x8007C114 - 0x8007C62C with globals such as func_8007C114, func_8007C17C, func_8007C224, func_8007C2E0, and func_8007C4BC.

Moved many character-specific assembly files from asm/melee/ft/ into asm/melee/ft/chara/ as 100% similarity renames, including ftmario.s, ftfox.s, ftlink.s, ftkirby.s, ftmasterhand.s, and many others.
Updated obj_files.mk to reflect the reorganized fighter assembly object list and new paths.
smart_moves: Used 100% similarity renames for character files, keeping behavior unchanged while making the directory layout more navigable.

Separated broad fighter assembly into topic-named units such as ftanim, ftcoll, ftcommon, ftparts, ftaction, and ftcamera, which makes later decompilation and symbol triage easier than working inside one monolithic code_800804EC.s file.
Preserved address-range style fallback names such as code_8007C114.s and code_8007C630.s where stronger semantic names were not evident, avoiding invented names.
Updated the object-file manifest in the same PR as the assembly movement, reducing the risk of build breakage after file relocation.
decomp_lessons: Large assembly files can be split mechanically before full C decompilation; this improves ownership and searchability without requiring semantic certainty for every function.

When function purpose is unclear, prefer address-based filenames for unsorted chunks and use semantic filenames only for groups with enough evidence, as seen with code_8007C114.s alongside ftanim.s, ftcoll.s, and ftparts.s.
Directory organization matters for long-running decomp projects: placing character-specific fighter assembly under asm/melee/ft/chara/ separates per-character work from common fighter systems.
Make build manifest updates part of any file split or rename PR so object ordering and paths remain explicit.
A PR can intentionally be an incremental cleanup; the body notes the remaining code is still large and needs more splitting, making this a staging step for later decomp work.
naming_conventions: Common fighter subsystem assembly files use the ft prefix plus subsystem noun: ftaction.s, ftanim.s, ftcamera.s, ftcoll.s, ftcommon.s, ftparts.s.
Character-specific fighter assembly files keep the ft character-name prefix and are grouped under asm/melee/ft/chara/.
Unclassified or address-bounded chunks retain code_XXXXXXXX.s names, e.g. code_8007C114.s and code_8007C630.s.
Existing internal assembly symbols remain raw address-derived names in the excerpt, such as func_8007C114 and lbl_8007C154; the PR does not provide evidence of semantic symbol renaming.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Mechanical assembly relocation and splitting rather than behavioral source changes; the diff is dominated by lines removed from code_800804EC.s and re-added to smaller .s files.
Character assembly files were moved with 100% similarity, indicating exact-content preservation during directory reorganization.
New assembly files retain .include "macros.inc", .section .text address comments, .global declarations, original instruction comments, and original label/function names.
The split appears to preserve address ordering/ranges for unresolved assembly chunks, which is important for matching and object layout stability.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body itself provides the main caveat: the file split is only a start and the remaining fighter assembly is still large and needs more splitting.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue splitting asm/melee/ft/code_800804EC.s or the new ftcommon/ftcoll/ftanim files.
Search for later semantic renames of func_8007C114, func_8007C17C, func_8007C224, func_8007C2E0, and func_8007C4BC.
Check whether asm/melee/ft/chara/ remained the stable location for character-specific fighter assembly in later build manifests.
Look for subsequent PRs that convert ftaction.s, ftanim.s, ftcamera.s, ftcoll.s, ftcommon.s, or ftparts.s from assembly into C.
